summary: solidity-deploy-guard ships wallet/credential scanner with hardcoded C2 exfiltration
details: The package contains three modules with active-attack fingerprints. wallet-scanner.js (1300+ lines) combines child_process, fs, https, and curl invocations with POST exfiltration calls — the classic crypto-wallet enumeration shape. scanner.js hardcodes a C2 endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (a personal GitHub Pages site, not a publisher domain), constructs system-intelligence payloads from os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), and current working directory, and POSTs them via https.request. build-verifier.js similarly composes os.hostname()/username/cwd into a JSON body and POSTs over https, while also invoking execSync('docker push...'). The combination of (a) hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 on a github.io page, (b) host/user/cwd intelligence collection, (c) a dedicated wallet-scanner module, and (d) curl-shell exfiltration paths is unambiguous installer-side credential and wallet theft. Package name implies a Solidity deployment safety tool; the shipped functionality is a wallet/host stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
